AUS.name: Patrick BEACH
AUS.highlights: Extraordinary clean sheet performance — 31 total attempts faced, 7 on target conceded, 0 goals. Commanded penalty area decisively with 75 total involvements and 20 GK line breaks.

AUS.name: Nestory IRANKUNDA
AUS.highlights: First-half impact: scored Australia's opener at 26' with a clinical ball-progression finish and drew another save at 44' before being withdrawn at 61'. Highest zone-5 sprint distance (296m) on either team.

AUS.name: Connor METCALFE
AUS.highlights: Sealed the match at 74' with a composed loose-ball finish. Team-high distance (11.6km), most high-speed runs (150), and crucial defensive tenacity with 3 tackles and 7 blocks despite playing the full 90.

AUS.name: Jordan BOS
AUS.highlights: Match top speed at 36.7 km/h. Six completed line breaks from left back (100% rate). Constant outlet in transition that stretched TUR's defensive structure.

TUR.name: Arda GULER
TUR.highlights: Most attempts (8) and most line breaks completed (22). Orchestrated TUR's sustained possession phases — 116 total offers made, 45 received — but failed to convert despite 3 on-target shots.

TUR.name: Hakan CALHANOGLU
TUR.highlights: Volume machine: 103 passes attempted (91% completion), 3 switches of play, 5 free kick deliveries. Two direct free-kick attempts on goal; one on target at 85'.

TUR.name: Ferdi KADIOGLU
TUR.highlights: Left-back engine: 109 passes (highest on pitch), 39 line breaks attempted, 35 completed (90%). Constant threat down the left flank with 3 open-play crosses.

TUR.name: Kenan YILDIZ
TUR.highlights: Immediate substitute impact — 6 attempts at goal (most in-match after coming on at HT), 7 take-ons. Kept TUR's attack threatening in the second half despite the scoreline.

AUS.summary: Australia executed a disciplined low-block counter-attack plan to perfection. Sitting deep in a compact 5-3-2 (56 sequences in low-block), they absorbed TUR's 63% possession and struck on transitions. Both goals came from quick progression rather than sustained pressure.
strengths: Defensive compactness: low-block structure frustrated TUR's intricate build-up, forcing 30 shots but only 7 on target
Transition speed: IRANKUNDA and METCALFE were decisive on the counter, each scoring from direct forward actions
Goalkeeper heroics: BEACH provided an elite safety net — 100% save rate against a bombardment from the more technically gifted opponent
Physical intensity: AUS out-ran TUR in total distance (116.6 vs 112.5 km) despite having less possession, demonstrating superior aerobic output
weaknesses: Minimal possession (27%): reliance on defensive structure leaves no margin for error if early goals are conceded
Low cross completion (3/9 open play): limited quality in wide deliveries when AUS did venture forward
Vulnerable to wave attacks: TUR's 30 total shots and sustained pressure could have broken through with sharper finishing

TUR.summary: Türkiye dominated the ball (63%) and created more expected goals (1.66 xG) but could not convert against a disciplined AUS defensive block. An overreliance on volume possession without sufficient vertical penetration led to a frustrating loss.
strengths: Possession quality: 92% pass completion (722/663) with fluid circulation through CALHANOGLU, BARDAKCI, and KADIOGLU
Set-piece threat: 8 corners and 12 direct free kicks created repeated danger — CALHANOGLU and GULER delivered threatening deliveries
Attacking movement: GULER's 116 total offers and AKTURKOGLU's deep runs constantly tested the AUS backline
Line-break quality: 132 completed line breaks (vs AUS's 56) showed TUR's ability to progress into dangerous areas
weaknesses: Finishing: 30 shots yielded 0 goals — poor conversion against a compact low-block despite 1.66 xG
Counter-attack vulnerability: gaps on the break conceded two goals from just 9 AUS shots; high line exploited
Ineffective crossing: only 1 of 22 open-play crosses completed (5% completion rate) — width was wasted
Tactical rigidity: struggled to break down the low block with a second-half rotation to YILDIZ offering more shots but not the creative disruption needed
